title: ED bounce — missed opportunity
scenarioBrief: Ten months later, Maria presents to the ED (2026-11-04). You are covering night float and asked to triage and decide on disposition. Review the chart and respond.

chartGate._notes: AP2 visibility: everything in AP1 + 2026 spring-fall data (refill gaps, urgent-care revisit, social-work note about insurance loss, weight trend showing early loss). No 2027 data yet.

prompts.question: What is your immediate disposition and workup plan?

essential: Restart hydroxychloroquine and address the adherence barrier (medication access, refill plan)
Re-engage rheumatology — concrete plan to get the referral honored despite insurance barriers
Workup for weight loss separately from the rheum picture (CMP, prealbumin/albumin, infection screen, thyroid recheck)
Social work consult for insurance navigation and food access
Language-concordant follow-up scheduling and clear return precautions
bonus: Explicitly identifies the AP1 visit as a missed opportunity that contributed to this state
Proactive dysphagia or swallow screen given the constitutional picture
Depression / mental-health screen given chronic illness + social stress
TB consideration revisited given progressive constitutional symptoms
redFlags: Discharge home without scheduled follow-up or a plan to address adherence
Anchoring only on 'SLE flare' and giving a steroid burst without firm diagnosis
Ignoring the weight loss as 'expected with chronic illness'
Starting new immunosuppression without a TB / infection screen
Not involving social work despite obvious social drivers
